key_points: Umbrella review of 9 systematic reviews evaluating laser-activated irrigation (LAI) in endodontics, covering Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG, Nd:YAG, and diode laser systems.
Available evidence on laser-activated irrigation remains limited and heterogeneous; findings are inconsistent across included systematic reviews.
Some systematic reviews suggest potential benefits in canal disinfection and postoperative outcomes, but results are not consistent.
Major sources of heterogeneity include variability in laser wavelengths, study designs, and outcome measures.
Laser-assisted irrigation should currently be regarded as an adjunct with unproven clinical benefit, pending robust evidence from well-designed studies with standardised protocols.
Further standardised, high-quality clinical research is required before definitive conclusions on clinical effectiveness can be established.
Methodological quality of included systematic reviews was assessed using the AMSTAR 2 tool.
Review registered at PROSPERO (CRD42025599352); searched PubMed/MEDLINE, Cochrane, SciELO, Web of Science, and LILACS through December 2024.

outcomes.result: Some systematic reviews suggest potential benefits, but findings are inconsistent across studies
outcomes.clinical_significance: Insufficient evidence to confirm clinical benefit of laser-activated irrigation for canal disinfection

outcomes.result: Some systematic reviews suggest potential benefits, but evidence is heterogeneous and inconclusive
outcomes.clinical_significance: No definitive conclusions can be drawn regarding postoperative pain or flare-up reduction

notes: This is an umbrella review (review of systematic reviews). Only 9 of 319 initially identified systematic reviews met eligibility criteria after exclusion of duplicates, in vitro-only studies, and studies with limited clinical relevance. The review focuses on primary endodontic cases.

population.condition: Primary endodontic cases requiring root canal treatment

source_citation: Duarte, M. A., Neves, J. A., Caçador, M., & Flores-Fraile, J. (2026). Clinical Efficiency of Lasers in Endodontic Treatment of Primary Endodontic Cases: An Umbrella Review of Systematic Reviews. International Dental Journal. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.identj.2026.109470
clinical_indication: Root canal irrigation and disinfection in primary endodontic cases
target_tissue: root canal (pulp chamber and canal walls)
uncertainties: No specific laser parameters (wavelength settings, power, fluence, pulse duration) are reported in the abstract; these may be present in the full text of included systematic reviews but are not extractable from the available abstract.
Conflicts of interest and funding sources are not declared in the available abstract.
The exact AMSTAR 2 quality ratings for each included systematic review are not available from the abstract alone.
Number of participants across included studies is not reported in the abstract.
